settings_category_user_desc: Runtime settings for user-facing account features such as avatars, sign-up, and future third-party login options.
settings_category_auth_desc: Cookie security policy plus access-token and refresh-token lifetimes.
settings_category_mail_desc: SMTP and sender settings for registration activation, email change confirmation, and other transactional mail.
settings_subcategory_mail_config_desc: SMTP connectivity, sender identity, and test delivery for transactional email.
settings_subcategory_mail_template_desc: Subjects and HTML bodies for registration, password reset, and email change messages. View magic variables next to the corresponding HTML body field.
settings_category_network_desc: Cross-origin access rules, preflight caching, and related network behavior.
settings_category_operations_desc: Runtime knobs for dispatch cadence, maintenance scheduling, and administrative list limits.
settings_subcategory_operations_backgrou: Separate concurrency caps for background task lanes so slow archive work cannot block quick thumbnail jobs.
settings_category_storage_desc: Version retention, cleanup policies, and default storage quota.
settings_category_webdav_desc: Control whether the WebDAV entrypoint is exposed.
settings_category_audit_desc: Audit logging switches and retention windows.
settings_category_general_desc: Site-wide public branding and shared deployment behavior.
settings_category_other_desc: System settings that do not belong to a named category.
settings_item_webdav_enabled_desc: Controls whether the WebDAV entrypoint is exposed. When disabled, clients can no longer access files through WebDAV.
settings_item_webdav_block_system_files_: Prevents WebDAV clients from creating common operating-system metadata files and folders such as .DS_Store, Thumbs.db, and desktop.ini.
settings_item_webdav_block_system_file_p: Basename patterns rejected during WebDAV writes. Simple * wildcards are supported and matching is case-insensitive.
settings_subcategory_general_preview_des: Manage the public preview app registry exposed to the frontend. This section edits the same JSON config using a structured list UI.
settings_subcategory_storage_archive_ext: Limit online archive extraction by source size, expanded size, entry count, path shape, and compression ratio to reduce archive-bomb and metadata-exhaustion risk.
settings_subcategory_storage_archive_pre: Control read-only archive manifest preview availability and cap source size, entry count, response size, and scan duration.
settings_subcategory_storage_archive_bui: Limit expanded entry count, total source bytes, and ZIP output size for batch compression and archive downloads.
settings_subcategory_storage_media_proce: Configure media metadata extraction, thumbnail processors, and safety limits.
settings_save_hint: Changes are staged as drafts until you save. Review them, then save together with ⌘/Ctrl + S.
custom_config_empty: No custom config yet. Add entries here only when an additional override is necessary.
custom_config_key_required: New custom config entries must include a key.
custom_config_key_duplicate: That config key already exists. Use a different name.
custom_config_validation_error: Fix empty or duplicate custom config keys before saving.
custom_config_delete_staged: These custom config entries are marked for deletion and will only be removed after you save.
config_namespace_hint: Use namespace.name format, for example integrations.slack_theme
